contentBlocks.original: अस्य श्री चन्द्र कवचस्य । गौतम ऋषिः । अनुष्टुप् छन्दः । श्री चन्द्रो देवता । चन्द्र प्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: asya śrī chandra kavachasya । gautama ṛṣiḥ । anuṣṭup Chandaḥ । śrī chandrō dēvatā । chandra prītyarthē japē viniyōgaḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dedication (Viniyoga): Of this Śrī Chandra Kavacham, Gautama is the sage, Anuṣṭup is the metre, Śrī Chandra is the presiding deity. It is dedicated to recitation for the grace of Chandra.

contentBlocks.original:
समं चतुर्भुजं वन्दे केयूर मकुटोज्वलम् ।
वासुदेवस्य नयनं शङ्करस्य च भूषणम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samaṃ chaturbhujaṃ vandē kēyūra makuṭōjvalam ।
vāsudēvasya nayanaṃ śaṅkarasya cha bhūṣaṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the serene, four-armed Moon, resplendent with armlets and crown, the eye of Vāsudeva and the ornament of Śaṅkara.

contentBlocks.original: एवं ध्यात्वा जपेन्नित्यं शशिनः कवचं शुभम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: ēvaṃ dhyātvā japēnnityaṃ śaśinaḥ kavachaṃ śubham ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having thus meditated, one should daily recite the auspicious armor of the Moon.
Now the Chandra Kavacham (Armor of the Moon):

contentBlocks.original:
शशी पातु शिरोदेशं भालं पातु कलानिधिः ।
चक्षुषी चन्द्रमाः पातु श्रुती पातु निशापतिः ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaśī pātu śirōdēśaṃ bhālaṃ pātu kalānidhiḥ ।
chakṣuṣī chandramāḥ pātu śrutī pātu niśāpatiḥ ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May Śaśī protect my head; may Kalānidhī protect my forehead; may Chandramā protect my eyes; may Niśāpati protect my ears. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
प्राणं क्षपकरः पातु मुखं कुमुदबान्धवः ।
पातु कण्ठं च मे सोमः स्कन्धे जैवातृकस्तथा ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prāṇaṃ kṣapakaraḥ pātu mukhaṃ kumudabāndhavaḥ ।
pātu kaṇṭhaṃ cha mē sōmaḥ skandhē jaivātṛkastathā ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May Kṣapākara protect my vital breath; may Kumudabāndhava (friend of the water lily) protect my face; may Soma protect my throat; may Jaivātṛka protect my shoulders. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
करौ सुधाकरः पातु वक्षः पातु निशाकरः ।
हृदयं पातु मे चन्द्रो नाभिं शङ्करभूषणः ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karau sudhākaraḥ pātu vakṣaḥ pātu niśākaraḥ ।
hṛdayaṃ pātu mē chandrō nābhiṃ śaṅkarabhūṣaṇaḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May Sudhākara (bearer of nectar) protect my hands; may Niśākara (maker of night) protect my chest; may Chandra protect my heart; may Śaṅkarabhūṣaṇa (ornament of Śiva) protect my navel. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
मध्यं पातु सुरश्रेष्ठः कटिं पातु सुधाकरः ।
ऊरू तारापतिः पातु मृगाङ्को जानुनी सदा ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
madhyaṃ pātu suraśrēṣṭhaḥ kaṭiṃ pātu sudhākaraḥ ।
ūrū tārāpatiḥ pātu mṛgāṅkō jānunī sadā ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the best among the gods protect my waist; may Sudhākara protect my hips; may Tārāpati (lord of stars) protect my thighs; may Mṛgāṅka always protect my knees. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
अब्धिजः पातु मे जङ्घे पातु पादौ विधुः सदा ।
सर्वाण्यन्यानि चाङ्गानि पातु चन्द्रोखिलं वपुः ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
abdhijaḥ pātu mē jaṅghē pātu pādau vidhuḥ sadā ।
sarvāṇyanyāni chāṅgāni pātu chandrōkhilaṃ vapuḥ ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May Abdhija (born of the ocean) protect my calves; may Vidhu always protect my feet; may Chandra protect all other limbs and my entire body. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
फलश्रुतिः
एतद्धि कवचं दिव्यं भुक्ति मुक्ति प्रदायकम् ।
यः पठेच्छृणुयाद्वापि सर्वत्र विजयी भवेत् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
phalaśrutiḥ
ētaddhi kavachaṃ divyaṃ bhukti mukti pradāyakam ।
yaḥ paṭhēchChṛṇuyādvāpi sarvatra vijayī bhavēt ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Fruits of Recitation (Phalaśruti): This divine armor bestows both enjoyment and liberation. Whoever recites or even hears it shall be victorious everywhere. (6)
Thus ends the Śrī Chandra Kavacham.
